reviews.name: Eye Value
reviews.text:
⚠️ URGENT TRAVEL WARNING: DO NOT BOOK THIS HOTEL

I am writing this as a heartbroken and frustrated traveler to save anyone else from the nightmare I just experienced. If you are planning a trip to Goa with friends or family, avoid this property at all costs.
🚩 The "Scam" Cycle

This isn't just a bad hotel; it’s a calculated fraud. Since 2024, I have tracked this property changing its name four different times to wipe away hundreds of 1-star reviews and trick new victims. They have even falsified their Google Maps location to appear closer to the action, but in reality, it is nowhere near Vagator or the main party hubs.
🏨 Why This is a Disaster:

The "Catfish" Photos: The professional, beautified photos you see on booking sites are a complete lie. The reality is a decaying building that looks nothing like the advertisements.

Biohazard Washrooms: The bathrooms are beyond "dirty"—they are unsanitary and disgusting. Expect broken fixtures, foul smells, and grime that hasn't been touched in months.

The "Swamp" Pool: The swimming pool is a green, murky mess. It is a health hazard and completely unusable.

Dilapidated Rooms: The rooms are overpriced, poorly maintained, and feel like they haven't been cleaned in years.

Zero Connectivity: Because they faked their location, you will spend a fortune on taxis just to get to the actual beaches or clubs in North Goa.

Final Verdict: This place is a trap designed to steal your money and spoil your vacation memories. I lost my peace of mind and my hard-earned money here. Please, do your research, check the history of the "new" names popping up on maps, and do not book this hotel under any condition.

updatesFromCustomers.text:
We booked this resort through MMT (MakeMyTrip) and stayed in the Luxurious Wooden Cottage with Garden View. Overall, it was a comfortable experience with a few areas that need improvement.
Breakfast:
The breakfast provided was good and tasty. However, it’s important to go early—if you arrive late, you may miss better milk and a few food items.
TV: The TV could not be operated because the account was not charged, which was a bit disappointing.
The swimming pool was accessible and clean.
Overall:
The resort provides a pleasant stay with good amenities. Fixing the TV issue and making wellness facilities operational would improve the experience further.

updatesFromCustomers.text: Came here for work on 19th January 2026 from Kerala after a week of talking over call and him booking a early morning flight for me. Wife called me unaware of this it seems with no proper solution after 3 hours. Told me will talk to owner and let me know. Left the same evening via flight. Incurred major emotional distress and financial loss due to same. Been more than week but no response from owners end till now all messages ignored. I hope they treat staff in a more human manner.

reviews.name: Sharayu Gaikwad
reviews.text: Beautiful property but a very bad experience during check in. Made us pay again for a prepaid room, not just ours, tried to make us pay for another room occupied by family which was similarly already paid for and after more than a week, the amount has not been refunded. Also, they treat you as if you can't afford to stay with them. Bad front desk behaviour and over charging and not refunding is the cherry on the top

reviews.name: Rahul Jain
reviews.text:
Stayed here for a single night and had a very pleasant experience overall. The room was extremely spacious and came with a large private balcony. The reception staff was courteous and personally escorted us to the room, taking the time to explain the in-room amenities and features.
The room itself felt luxurious, with rich finishes and thoughtful additions such as a coffee maker. The bathroom was particularly impressive, featuring a sunken bathtub as well as an outdoor shower. The water pressure was excellent, making the warm shower especially enjoyable.
The hotel’s Portuguese-inspired architecture, with its piazzas and charming bridges, adds to the character of the property. It also offers direct access to Arrosim Beach, which is pristine and peaceful. We truly enjoyed spending time there during sunset.
There is also a specialty Royal Afghan restaurant located by the beach. We opted for the buffet dinner, and the food quality and flavours were excellent. Thoroughly enjoyed the starters, main course and dessert. Special mention for the tasteful pav bhaji and the bajara phirni.
The only downside during our stay was the spa at Kaya Kalp. The spa beds were made of wood/cane and were not very comfortable, and the spa staff did not seem particularly well trained. This is one area where the hotel could certainly improve.
The staff overall was attentive and helpful throughout our stay. The buffet breakfast was very sumptuous, with a wide variety of options.
Overall, we had a wonderful stay and would highly recommend the property.

title: The Byke Heritage - Old Anchor (South Goa)
description: Relaxed resort offering laid-back rooms, plus dining, bars, an outdoor pool & a gym.
address: Cavelossim, Beach, Mobor Beach, Goa 403731, India
hotelDescription:
This laid-back resort is a 3-minute walk from Cavelossim Beach. It's also 16 km from the Holy Spirit Church and 19 km from Cabo de Rama, a ruined fort.
 Offering Wi-Fi, the unassuming rooms also have minibars and flat-screen TVs with cable channels, as well as tea and coffeemaking facilities. Upgraded rooms add seating areas and balconies, while suites have separate living rooms.
 Breakfast is served in a vegetarian restaurant. There are also 3 bars. Other amenities include an outdoor pool, a children's play area, a gym and a spa, plus a game room, an on-site doctor and a back-up power generator.

ownerUpdates.text:
Holi is not just about colours in the air. It is about families gathering, friends reconnecting, and laughter echoing through familiar spaces. 

At The Byke Hotels and Resorts, we understand that meaningful celebrations do not need to be extravagant. They need to be heartfelt.
As a budget hotel built on comfort and care, we are proud to host moments that feel simple, safe, and memorable. This Holi, may your life be painted with happiness, peace, and togetherness.
Wishing you a joyful and colourful celebration.

Choosing a Hotel in South Goa is not just about convenience. It is about how your day flows.

At Byke, our properties are set in prime locations so you spend less time commuting and more time experiencing. When your hotel sits close to key attractions, business hubs or city centres, mornings feel unhurried and evenings stay open ended.

It means easier sightseeing, quicker meetings, better dining access, and the freedom to return and recharge whenever you want.  No rigid schedules. No wasted hours on the road.
So why delay? Prefer the Byke stay and experience easy hotel booking, ensuring everything feels sorted even before you arrive.

A good budget hotel is more than a place to sleep; it’s where your day finds its rhythm. 
Clean, hygienic rooms and comfortable beds help you wake refreshed and rest deeply. 
Thoughtful meals fuel your adventures without slowing you down. 
Every detail, from quiet spaces to attentive service, is designed to let you move at your own pace. 
So you never regret the hotel booking and miss home.

True luxury is not about stiffness, silence, or a long list of rules pinned behind a desk. A real luxury hotel experience is where comfort comes with warmth and structure leaves space for understanding.
At The Byke, we believe luxury begins with listening. We value conversations more than rulebooks and people more than processes. Travel plans change, moods shift, and needs are rarely identical. That is why our approach to luxury hospitality is human first.

Late arrival after a long journey. A special food request. A quiet corner instead of a formal setup. These are not exceptions for us. They are part of how a luxury hotel stay should feel. Flexible, respectful, and considerate of your time.

Our teams are trained to understand intent, not just instructions. Instead of rigid checklists, we focus on comfort, clarity, and care. When you stay with us, you are not navigating policies. You are having conversations. And those conversations shape your experience.
Because true luxury is not about enforcing rules.
 It is about knowing when to soften them.

A good hotel does more than give you a place to sleep. It quietly sets the rhythm for your entire day. When your hotel booking is right, mornings do not feel rushed and evenings do not feel exhausting.
At a good hotel, the day begins with comfort you can count on. Clean and hygienic rooms that let you rest deeply. Thoughtful food options that fuel you without slowing you down. Services that respond before you have to ask. Nothing loud. Nothing forced. Just ease.
A convenient location means less time stuck in traffic and more time exploring, working, or unwinding. Whether you step out early for meetings or start slow with breakfast and views, the stay adapts to your pace, not the other way around.
That is the real value of choosing the right hotel booking. When the stay works smoothly in the background, your plans flow better, your energy lasts longer, and travel feels lighter.

We know that not every journey feels the same. Some trips are about ease where you need smooth check ins, restful rooms, and everything working quietly in the background. Other journeys are about experience where the surroundings, the food, and the feeling of the place matter just as much as the destination. 
As a trusted hotel in South Goa, we design our stays with this understanding at the core. Whether you are travelling for work, family time, or personal moments of pause, we focus on comfort, clarity, and care so your stay feels effortless and meaningful from the moment you arrive.

This Republic Day, The Byke Hotels and Resorts stands with every Indian in honouring the values that shape our Republic. Equality, dignity, and the shared responsibility of building a nation where everyone belongs.
Across our properties, from busy city centres to peaceful holiday destinations, we reflect these ideals in the way we welcome, serve, and care for every guest. Every smile, every meal, and every stay is guided by the belief that respect and inclusion are the heart of true hospitality.That makes any hotel a luxury hotel in true sense. 
To every traveller, guest, and team member, thank you for being part of our journey. You bring the spirit of India into our spaces, turning simple stays into meaningful connections.
Happy Republic Day from everyone at The Byke.
Jai Hind.

Makar Sankranti, known as Pongal in the south, Uttarayan in Gujarat, and Lohri in Punjab, is a festival of harvest, kites, and joyful traditions.

As you celebrate with family and friends, may every moment be full of warmth, laughter, and happiness. And whenever your travels take you far from home, may you always find comfort and ease in a good hotel to rest and recharge.
From all of us at The Byke, wishing you a Makar Sankranti that’s as vibrant and uplifting as the kites in the sky!

Happy New Year! ✨
May 2026 bring you more reasons to celebrate  and more reasons to travel. When you do, choose a Budget Hotel that keeps things simple: clean rooms, friendly service, and a hassle-free stay.
From all of us at The Byke, wishing you a New Year filled with warmth, comfort, and beautiful beginnings.

Winter is calling, and there’s no better way to welcome it than with a warm, thoughtful stay at a hotel in South Goa. At The Byke, every stay is designed to make your winter escape truly special.
Imagine waking up to scenic views, enjoying slow, peaceful mornings, and savoring a hot, homely breakfast in cosy rooms crafted for comfort. Whether your heart is set on the crisp mountain air, the mild winter sun along the coast, or a serene spiritual retreat, The Byke has the perfect space to match your mood.
Our pure veg hospitality ensures every meal delights, while our attention to comfort, care, and warmth makes your winter getaway effortless and memorable.
This season, step into winter with relaxation, charm, and a stay that feels like home.

description: Warmly furnished rooms in an upscale hotel offering dining, an outdoor pool & a spa, plus a dog run.

hotelDescription:
Set amid rainforest, this upmarket hotel is 10 km from Abbey Falls, 7 km from Madikeri Fort and 95 km from Kannur International Airport.
 Airy, warmly furnished rooms provide smart TVs, en suite bathrooms and Wi-Fi, as well as minifridges and air conditioning. Upgraded rooms add terraces. Pet-friendly rooms add dog beds and toys.

updatesFromCustomers.text: Little G

ownerUpdates.text: A memorable trip to the Scotland of India must begin with choosing the right place to stay in Coorg. Gateway Coorg has created the perfect base for business and leisure travellers. The resort presents refined living with the unspoiled essence of Kodagu, along with suites that frame nature like a painting. It becomes a starting point for journeys into Coorg’s heritage and landscapes. From treks through lush hills to historical sites, the property offers you the best of Coorg. With dining that caters to varied tastes and a location that connects you to cultural gems, Gateway Coorg transforms your holiday. It is the complete Coorg experience.

Amid the several choices of luxury hotels in Coorg, Gateway Coorg offers an exceptional stay. The property features verdant landscapes, fragrant coffee estates nearby, and restful suites within. Every detail is tailored to bring comfort and luxury together. Enjoy easy access to Coorg’s temples, treks, and traditions. Days may be spent walking scenic paths, visiting heritage sites, or simply enjoying the calm of a private balcony. The hotel’s multi-cuisine offerings are the topping on the cake, presenting flavours both familiar and new. At Gateway Coorg, we wait to serve.

Look forward to every meal at Gateway’s Ghats Café, a global cuisine restaurant in Coorg. At Gateway Coorg, the Ghats Café proves that great taste needs to be carefully curated and has no borders. Here, meticulously prepared Kodava specialities sit alongside must-try Indian staples and internationally inspired favourites. It’s casual, it’s refined, and it’s consistently surprising. For a dining experience that breaks the mould without losing its roots, your table is waiting at Gateway Coorg. Book a seat to step inside and savour the global cuisine at the Ghats Café.

Wake up in the heart of Coorg’s rolling hills at Gateway. It’s the best place to stay in Coorg for those who appreciate nature and comfort in equal measure. The rainforest trails, stunning views from every corner and aromatic breezes set the stage for a refreshing escape. Culinary delights inspired by Coorg’s heritage and traditional wellness treatments await you. A stay at Gateway Coorg offers both your mind and body the much needed rest it deserves. Surrounded by Coorg’s natural beauty, it's the ideal place to slow down and recharge. Ready to unwind in style? Book your stay and experience it firsthand.

Swap your daily routine for tranquillity at Gateway Coorg, where luxury meets nature in a setting renowned for its beauty. Amongst the top luxury hotels in Coorg, this oasis is designed for those who appreciate authenticity and unmatched service. Revel in local and global cuisines, unwind in our serene wellness zones, and wake up each day to the dulcet sounds of forest life. Stroll through heritage sites or simply do nothing at all – it’s your story to write. Secure your reservation now for a taste of true Coorg indulgence.

reviews.name: Shruthi B M
reviews.text:
Booked Gateway Coorg for a staycation and we absolutely loved it! Would definitely come back, soon.

We spent two nights here as a family of 5. Spacious rooms with balcony overlooking their beautiful garden and included amazing spread for the breakfast. A la carte options and buffet for lunch/dinner included local options which were recommended by Ms.Katha and we were delighted. Ms.Katha and team took great care of our requirements and we truly appreciate the care & love.

Unique experiences included story telling sessions by the bonfire by Mr.Ganesh and Mr.Anoop who were highly knowledgeable of the local flora and fauna, and described in detail the origin of river Kaveri.

All in all, a few others I would like to mention for their kindness and hospitality are Ms.Riya and Ms.Shiwani from front desk, the valet team and everyone who truly ensured our comfort throughout the stay and made it very memorable for us!

Regards,
Shruthi B M & family

description: Serene forest property offering cottages & elegant villas with plunge pools, plus dining & a spa.
address: 123 Ibnivalavadi Village, Boikeri, Madikeri, Madikeri, Karnataka 571201, India
hotelDescription:
Amid lush forest, this serene resort on a former coffee plantation is 4 km from the Hindu Omkareshwara Temple, 5 km from the 17th-century Madikeri Fort and 6 km from the Raja's Seat, a seasonal garden.
 Stilted wood cottages are elegantly furnished, and offer flat-screens and free Wi-Fi, plus balconies with forest views. Polished villas add private plunge pools, whirlpool tubs and minibars.
 Complimentary perks include breakfast, lunch and dinner, plus parking and guided walks. Other amenities include 3 restaurants (1 is vegetarian; another is open-air), plus a bar, an outdoor pool and a spa. There's also a quaint cafe set beside a lake.

reviews.name: abi varma
reviews.text:
A Fantastic Stay with a Few Friendly Suggestions.

I recently stayed at Coorg Cliffs Resort, and I must say, the experience was absolutely fantastic and totally worth the price! From the moment we checked in until the very end, the hospitality and service were just awesome. The room and the location are both simply awesome—you really feel the beauty of Coorg here.

The Highlights: What We Loved!

Heartwarming Hospitality: The staff here really knows how to win hearts. Everyone makes sure you are treated well. They are constantly smiling, checking in on you, and speaking to you like a known person—it feels like being among friends. They really make sure you are taken care of!

Dining Service is Simply Superb: Especially the food service—it’s just awesome. Even though it’s a buffet, staff members keep asking what you want and even serve dishes directly from the live counter to your table. That’s really a class apart! The food taste and variety are excellent, which many other resorts miss.

The Infinity Pool – A Total Vibe: Their infinity pool was awesome and we enjoyed it a lot! Since it’s an open-air pool, you might see some leaves or small insects, which is understandable in the hills. However, a little more frequent cleaning would be great since kids use it frequently.

Excellent Guest Management: We noticed the resort handled a private group gathering very professionally. Even though they were enjoying their own drinks, the staff ensured they were organized separately so there was no loud music or disturbance to other guests.

Kid-Friendly & Personal Connection: They connect with kids so well and make them feel right at home. A big plus for us was that most of the staff speak our language, so we could connect so easily.

Activities: The plantation walk was awesome and short—perfect to enjoy the greenery. The kids' park is also very good!

A Friendly Update on Expectations (Suggestions for Management)
We had such a wonderful time that we want to share a few "friendly updates" on things that could make the guest experience even more perfect:

Room Privacy & Comfort (Room 103): Since our room was right by the pool, there was a bit of a loss of privacy with people standing right near the balcony and the sound of shouting from the pool. Also, the spring mattresses caused some body pain at the corners, which made it a bit tough to get a proper sleep.

Bathroom Drainage: We noticed there is no water hole/drain in the toilet area itself. There is only a small pipe connecting the toilet and bath area, but it doesn't allow water to flow out at all. Some water accidentally spilled near the toilet (kids being kids!), and we had to deal with a wet floor the whole time as the water had nowhere to go.

Food Sensitivity: While the food was 10/10 for taste and variety, unfortunately, our whole family had a stomach upset and pain in the middle of the night. I noticed a few other reviews mentioned this too, so it might be something the kitchen team can double-check just to be safe!

Parking Care: Since the parking is open, our pure black car got fully covered in dust, leaves, and bird poop. It’s hard to find a car wash during a vacation, so we had to travel with a dirty car. A covered parking area would be a huge blessing!

Recreation: The outdoor badminton court is tough to use because of the sun and wind in the hills. If you keep this indoor, I bet you will have many more guests enjoying the game!

Small Essentials: We missed having hair conditioner in the toiletries, especially needed after a dip in the swimming pool!

Final Thoughts
If these small items are looked into, this resort will for sure be the most competitive and well-suited place in the area. We had a wonderful stay and will definitely visit again for sure! Thanks to all staffs who gave us a good memories.

reviews.name: ramesh ramanathan
reviews.text:
Very well laid out hotel in a very green landscape with different types of rooms. Staff friendly. I stayed for one night —in 7 pm and out 9am the next morn.
3 issues I want to point out.
1) when the power goes off ( and it does frequently) it takes more than 15 minutes to switch on the generator. Ideally it must be restored in 45 secs. Wonder why they don’t have an Automatic transfer switch ?
2) Wi-Fi is weak. And does not come one for quite some time after the power resumes.
3) bathroom floor ( shower) slippery. No grab bar and no hair drier ( strange in a hotel with 2 swimming pools).
4) right at check in they inform you — if you need hot water in the shower , pls turn the single lever converter to the left and allow the water to run. They did not say how much but I estimate atleast 3 buckets of water run out before you get hot water. The hotel is  Very insensitive to the environment.
Front office Staff are friendly and warm but not efficient. .
I repeat , the location and facilities are superb . Large spacious rooms.
They must get their act together.

Atlast only they called someone and informed next day they can arrange. Gaming area is very disappointing. Outdoor pool is ok.
The rooms are not comfortable as a star resort

Set in a wooded area, this tranquil resort in a valley with natural springs is 6 km from Hattihole waterfall, 18 km from NH275 national highway and 22 km from the museum in 17th-century Madikeri Fort.
 Cosy cottages feature balconies overlooking the valley; some offer panoramic views. Some are set on stilts, and/or have warm wood architecture, living rooms and terraces. All provide tea and coffeemaking facilities. Kids age 11 and under stay free.
 Meals are included. Activities like night safaris, hiking and riverside camping can be arranged for a fee.

reviews.name: Nivetha Ganesh
reviews.text: The best place to detox from digital life. But anyways they provide separate WiFi for each cottage. We stayed from Friday to Sunday for a short staycation. The service was very good and the receptionists were so patient with everyone. The morning activity of bird visit was really a new experience and so, we went again on the next day.  We had complementary breakfast which was too good and not buffet. So, we also had dinner there after a small fire pit. We had a relaxing spa experience there. We had a great time at this resort and will definitely plan a return visit.

reviews.name: Yashas YS
reviews.text: One of the best stays that I’ve had in recent days. Great hospitality by team and wonderful food by the chef. I would love to visit this place again .

reviews.name: Amit Ahlawat
reviews.text:
I stayed here for 2 nights and oh boy, I was in for a surprise!!
First the property is excellent, no doubt. But what blew my mind was the hospitality. My wife & son, were very well, infact I should say, thoroughly spoiled by the staff. Let me acknowledge the true heros of this property. Starting with the Chef- Soumya Das. He prepares THE best dishes. I am an adventurer and have been to hundreds of properties. Never had food anywhere, that can match the food here. Good menu and fingerlicking food. Want something that's not on the menu- no problems. Chef got you covered.
Then there is the front staff- Omesh, Danny & Satish. They will not only welcome you with a smile, they will take care of your entire day, with activities. Super attentive, polite & helpful.
While you sit for food- Nachappa & Srija will ensure your plate is never empty. Very fast & super efficient. Again, it's a delight to see, how the team works so efficiently.
Evening bornfire is a treat. I did birding, river walk, coracle ride. We didn't step out of the property. We spent entire 2 days here. Had all meals here.
The property is located at a steep entrance but they got you covered. You will be picked & dropped from the parking as soon as you ask for it. Everyone here has walkie-talkie and the communication is super fast. Don't expect proper network but every room has its own wifi, so watsapp works perfectly. Who needs network on a holiday.
I just hope, after this review, I still get bookings 😂 when I need to visit, because I'm not done with this place. I will surely return.

reviews.text:
⚠️ Scam Alert — Avoid This Place!

The owner tries to lure customers from Airbnb by asking them to cancel their booking and book directly with him. Please don’t fall for this trap! Once you reach the property, you will regret the decision.

The rooms are in terrible condition — stinky, full of seepage, extremely dirty, and even the bedsheets looked used. The door doesn’t lock properly, and the curtains are so dirty and short that anyone can see inside. I felt completely unsafe and uncomfortable throughout my stay.

I have wasted my money and my entire trip because of this place. Please save yourself the trouble and avoid booking here at all costs!

Well it was not quite a good experience for me and am forced to show my disappointment here otherwise it will go unnoticed.

Stay was located in wonderful location however the rooms and services were very bad I must say.

Thanks for the weekend happiness from Kava where we planned for a relaxation weekend and all these happened. 😡

hotelDescription:
Surrounded by countryside, this laid-back hostel is 8 km from both Hirekolale Lake and Chikkamagaluru railway station, and 3 km from Mahatma Gandhi Park.
 Simple dorms provide bunk beds, Wi-Fi and ceiling fans. Bathrooms are shared. Private, wood-panelled chalets have decks and sleep 2 guests. There are also rooms sleeping 4 people.

reviews.name: Rohit Shivapur
reviews.text: It was an amazing experience to celebrate new years at CoffeeShire. We were 8 people and a kid. We were all taken care of very well. Very tasty and good food. Host made us feel at home. Bonfire and Barbecue were arranged on prior request.

reviews.name: S D
reviews.text:
The property is really good—peaceful, well-maintained, and surrounded by beautiful greenery. The rooms were clean, comfortable, and perfect for a relaxing getaway.
The food was absolutely delicious—fresh, homely, and full of authentic flavors. A special mention to the chef for preparing such tasty meals and accommodating our preferences so thoughtfully. Every meal felt warm and satisfying.
What truly made the stay memorable was the owner—extremely helpful, friendly, and always available whenever we needed anything. Their hospitality made us feel completely at home.
Highly recommend this place if you’re looking for great food, warm hosts, and a comfortable stay in Chikkamagalur.

reviews.name: Anupama G S
reviews.text: The short getaway to Coffeeshire with my friend was a beautiful experience. Nishanth was a wonderful host and made sure we felt at home. The property is spacious and well maintained. The room with the balcony gave us breathtaking views of the surroundings. The stay coupled with excellent local cuisine and the drive around, especially to Devaramane hill range, made the trip a memorable experience! And if you are an animal lover, you will definitely love the dogs and Cleo, the warm and fuzzy cat 😊

reviews.name: farzana
reviews.text: My friend and I had a wonderful time at Coffeeshire. Nishant’s hospitality was out of this world. It was like sleep over at your friends place. Very well maintained property. Food was excellent. The staff was friendly and always ready to extend any support we needed. The laughter shared with Nishant is unforgettable. Definitely a place to visit with friends or family 💖💝

reviewContext.Rooms: Large spacious room. Lovely attached Balcony to enjoy your morning coffee or read books or hangout with friends:)

reviewContext.Food & drinks: Yummy homemade food. Was delicious. Hard to stop eating 😋

reviews.name: Bharat Chitale
reviews.text:
The days we spent with Nishant and his family at Coffeeshire will always be such a precious memory for us. We really did not want to leave. We have found, not new friends, but a family.

Mrs Gowda's exquisite cooking deserves a special mention. She will always have a place in our heart. I have not met a more accomplished, passionate and welcoming host as the Gowda family We will visit again soon!

And the rare sighting of a Chevrotain (Indian Mouse Deer) and White Bellied Woodpecker at the estate will be an indelible memory.

title: The Heights - Best Homestay in Chikmagalur
address: Mallandur Rd, Bandekallu gudda, Jakkanahalli, Karnataka 577101, India

reviews.name: Grace N Glory
reviews.text:
My recent stay at The Heights was nothing short of extraordinary. Nestled amidst breathtaking scenery at an impressive altitude of 4800 meters, this homestay offers a truly unforgettable experience.
The accommodations were impeccable, providing a cozy and comfortable atmosphere that perfectly complemented the serene surroundings. The views from our room were simply breathtaking, with panoramic vistas of the surrounding mountains and valleys.
But what truly made our stay exceptional was the warm hospitality of our host, Santhosh. His knowledge of the local area and his genuine care for our well-being ensured that we had everything we needed. Santhosh's recommendations for nearby attractions and his assistance in arranging activities were invaluable.
Whether you're seeking adventure, relaxation, or simply a chance to escape the hustle and bustle of city life, The Heights is the perfect destination. I highly recommend this homestay to anyone looking for a truly memorable experience in Chikmagalur.

reviews.name: Rushank Muthreja
reviews.text:
A visit to The Height was the highlight of our trip to Chikmagalur! There's no better way I can think of to end our footballing trip than relaxing at the height of The Heights with friends and an absolutely amazing host!

The view from it's 5,000 ft elevation was breathtaking and just what one needed after a (very) steep kilometer long treck! I can't say enough about the view - it was heavenly!

The homestay itself has been very tastefully done - lovely seating arrangements - with a great view, comfortable tables and chairs made from natural wood, rugged, yet cozy tents...

What better dinner than freshly prepared steamed rice and chicken curry in the 10 degree temperature at that elevation! Straight from the wood fire to your plate! One could tell that the ingredients were fresh and the curry had an absolutely perfect mix of spices! Took me back home!

The host, Mr Santosh, made us feel at home and treated our group of guys (all 40+) as his friends - personally cooking for us and ensuring we're comfortable and having a great time!  Thankyou Santosh for this most amazing and memorable experience!

The Height is highly recommended  for nature enthusiasts and those that want relax and rise above the world!

reviews.name: Shashank Hegde
reviews.text: A splendid hillside location, great hospitality, and a short walk to Bandekallugudda which offers a breathtaking panaromic view of the surrounding Western Ghats. Dress warmly!

reviews.name: kenette francis lobo
reviews.text: From the time we reached to The Heights, it was a great feeling. The host was very hospitable. This high peak in Chikmagalur has great view and the good weather. One of the must try place in Chikmagalur.

reviews.name: ATUL GUPTA
reviews.text:
SilverCloud is a a beautiful and cosy homestay nestled within multiple acres of coffee plantation. The location is great and provides a beautiful view of the coffee estate.
It is a great value for money in terms of its location and offerings. What stood out for us was the top notch service, notwithstanding the fact that only 2 staffs were managing the entire property during our stay. The caretaker Santhosh and the cook were extremely pleasant and always on their toes to make our experience a delightful one. The Malnad cuisine served with the most authentic preparation style was finger licking good and one could return to this homestay just for its food and overall service. The rooms were pretty spacious and clean with ample open space outside as well for recreational use. It is a great option to unwind on a great budget if you’re not looking for a luxurious option. We loved the overall experience we had here, thanks to Santhosh and cook bhaiya, and we highly recommended this place for a cosy and delightful experience.

hotelDescription:
Surrounded by jungle, this relaxed resort is 7 km from hiking trails at Aranamala Peak, 9 km from the scenic Soochipara Falls and 17 km from NH766 national highway.
 Simple, treehouse-style cottages come with flat-screen TVs, and balconies with jungle views; some feature skylights. Room service is available.
 Amenities include an international restaurant, a gym, and an Ayurvedic spa with steam baths. There's also a game room and a playground. Trekking guides are available, as is breakfast. A doctor is on call.

reviews.name: Malini Vishweswaran
reviews.text: Our stay at Petals Hotel was truly memorable and exceeded all our expectations. From the moment we arrived, we were welcomed with genuine warmth, care, and attention to detail that made us feel more like valued guests than customers.What stood out the most was the highly personalized service. The team took time to understand our specific needs and preferences and went out of their way to cater to them with patience and a smiling face.The food deserves special appreciation—fresh, delicious, and prepared with care.We left with wonderful memories and a deep sense of gratitude. Petals Hotel is not just a place to stay—it is a place where guests are genuinely cared for.

ownerUpdates.text: From ripples of calm to laughter that fills the air, even the simplest moments by our pool feel timeless.
Infused with the soul of Wayanad’s serene landscape, J Wellness Circle is a sanctuary that redefines restoration — blending healing rituals with mindful luxury.

This festive season, Taj Wayanad Resort & Spa, Kerala brings you “Festivals of India”, a specially designed offer with exclusive member savings. Enjoy the warmth of Taj hospitality, comfortable stays, and delightful dining at our hotel in Kerala. Celebrate special moments with your loved ones and make every occasion more joyful with Taj. Become a member to avail benefits and enjoy more from your festive experience. Book your festive stay today.

Some luxury is curated, some simply discovered.
At our hilltop haven, every view from your room becomes a quiet conversation with nature.
Tucked away in the quiet heart of Wayanad, the air carries a calming stillness — a gentle reminder of the serene beauty found in every step.
Your pause. Your peace. Your paradise — all right here.

reviews.name: Siddharth Jha
reviews.text: The experience at Taj Wayand with the executive office team of Bosch India was absolutely splendid. The service, the food (specially curated by the chef), the pool, the sauna was all just perfect. Couldn't have asked for a more relaxing time. We were a group of 8 from 11th - 13 th Feb 2026.

reviews.name: Sager Sakla
reviews.text:
We recently stayed at Taj Wayanad Resort & Spa. The ambience and natural surroundings are beautiful, and the property itself is very well designed. The location is peaceful and scenic.
However, the hospitality was not up to the Taj standard we expected. We did not receive any prior call regarding check-in coordination. At the restaurant, the service was not very attentive — at one point, we were unsure whether our order had even been placed as there was no proper follow-up.
During check-in, there were used plates and glasses kept outside a room in front of us, which did not create a good first impression. Also, there is no direct access to the lake near the hotel, which was slightly disappointing.
Overall, it was an okay stay mainly because of the ambience, but the service experience could definitely be improved.

reviews.text: Wi-Fi was very limited with low bandwidth. Was charged extra for early check-in on an hourly basis( almost 650₹ extra), which was not informed during booking . The space is small and not comfortable for families or groups. Overall, not a good experience.

reviews.name: Mayur kadam
reviews.text:
I had a very poor experience here and would not recommend this hotel, especially for travelers coming from outside the area.

At the time of arrival, we were clearly told that early check-in was allowed. However, after some time, a staff member came and demanded extra payment for early check-in, which was never mentioned initially. This felt misleading and unprofessional.

The owner’s behavior was also unacceptable. From the beginning, he spoke very rudely and even started shouting when we said we would not pay the unexpected early check-in charge. There was no polite or respectful communication.

Another major issue was the inconsistency between staff and owner — staff members would say one thing, and later the owner would say something completely different. This created confusion and distrust.

Other problems we faced:
- Wi-Fi was not working
- Rooms are built too close to each other, making it difficult to get proper sunlight and natural ventilation.
- Owner were not helpful and very rude

Overall, it felt unsafe and dishonest. I strongly advise other outsiders and travelers to be careful before booking here.

title: Kadkani River Resort
subTitle: ಕಾಡಕಣಿ ರಿವರ್ಸೈಡ್ ರೆಸಾರ್ಟ್, ಕೊಡಗು
description: Laid-back resort offering a putting green & an outdoor pool, plus dining & breakfast.
address: Hachinad, Pachat, Ammathi, Karnataka 571211, India
hotelDescription:
Overlooking the Kaveri River, this remote resort set amidst lush greenery is 1.7 km from the village of Hachinad.
 The low-key cottages include sitting areas and flat-screen TVs, plus minibars, and tea and coffeemaking facilities. Some include verandas with hammocks. Upgraded cottages include separate living rooms and outdoor dining areas.
 Breakfast is available. There's a restaurant with a thatched roof, as well as an outdoor pool, a lake and a putting green.

reviews.name: Deepak Jain
reviews.text: This resort perfectly blends adventure and serenity. Enjoy thrilling boat ride, relax by the sparkling pool, and wander through lush plantations. It’s an idyllic, refreshing escape for nature lovers.

reviews.name: Sanjith fabz
reviews.text:
stayed at Kadakani River Resort recently, and it turned out to be a perfect getaway in the heart of Coorg. Surrounded by lush greenery and the soothing sound of the river, the resort offers a calm and refreshing atmosphere that instantly puts you at ease.

The rooms were clean, spacious, and well maintained, with beautiful views that made waking up in the morning truly special. The entire property feels very peaceful and close to nature.

The staff were extremely courteous and helpful throughout my stay, always making sure everything was comfortable. The food was another highlight—delicious, homely, and prepared with great care, giving a lovely taste of authentic local flavors.

Spending time sitting by the river and enjoying the natural surroundings was the most memorable part of my stay. If you’re looking for a relaxing nature retreat with excellent hospitality, Kadakani River Resort is highly recommended. A wonderful place to unwind and reconnect with nature.

ownerUpdates.text: In a world where sustainable travel is rapidly becoming the need of the hour, Club Mahindra Resorts is setting an inspiring benchmark for the hospitality industry with its unwavering commitment to planet-positive practices. As one of India’s leading vacation ownership brands, Club Mahindra not only delivers memorable holidays to families but also does so with a deep-rooted sense of responsibility towards the environment.

When you think of the perfect family vacation, what comes to mind? A serene beachfront cottage, a cozy hillside studio, or a spacious apartment where everyone has room to relax? At Club Mahindra, no matter how you travel or who you travel with there’s a room designed just for you.

With 140+ top-notch resorts across India and abroad, Club Mahindra ensures that every holiday is more than a getaway, it's a lifestyle. Here's how you can choose the perfect room type that fits your family, your comfort, and your holiday mood.

title: Porcupine Castle Resort Coorg
description: Relaxed chalets & treetop rooms in an eco-chic resort with an outdoor pool & a casual restaurant.
address: Porcupine Castle Resort Road, Hanchikad,, Pollibetta, Karnataka 571215, India
hotelDescription:
On a 121-hectare coffee plantation overlooking mountains and valleys, this exotic, eco-chic resort is 20 km from the Coorg Golf Links.
 Relaxed, cosy chalets offer free Wi-Fi, flat-screen TVs, minifridges, and tea and coffeemaking equipment. All come with sofabeds, living/dining areas and balconies. Upgraded chalets add mountain or valley views. There are also 2 rustic-chic treetop rooms.
 Freebies include parking and breakfast, which is provided in a casual restaurant serving Asian cuisine. Other amenities consist of an outdoor pool and a kids' playground with a trampoline. Plantation tours are offered for a fee. Pets are welcome.

reviews.text:
We had a wonderful stay at Porcupine Resort. We visited to celebrate our anniversary and a close friend’s birthday, and the experience was absolutely memorable.

The staff were well-mannered and friendly. A special thanks to Manoj and Mani for going the extra mile to make our stay extra special. This was actually my second visit, and it was just as delightful as the first.

The scenery is breathtaking—calm, serene, and so close to nature. The cottages are comfortable and perfect for a peaceful getaway. The food was also excellent.both drinking water and in the pool—was well maintained and up to the mark.

Definitely recommend this resort to everyone!

subTitle: ಮ್ಯಾಗ್ನೋಲಿಯಾ ಎಸ್ಟೇಟ್ಸ್ & ರೆಸಾರ್ಟ್
description: Tranquil resort on a coffee plantation offering rustic quarters, a nature reserve & dining.
address: Arji Hills, Near Perumbadi Checkpost, Virajpet, Karnataka 571218, India
hotelDescription:
On 34 hectares of grounds that have a coffee plantation and a nature reserve, this tranquil resort off the NH 91 highway is 16 km from the Brahmagiri Wildlife Sanctuary.
 Featuring antique and upcycled furniture, the rustic cottages come with flat-screen TVs and minibars, as well as tea and coffeemaking facilities. All have living areas, as well as patios offering area views.
 Breakfast is served in an informal restaurant. There's also an alcohol-free bar; guests may bring their own alcohol. Other amenities include sports courts, a game room and a TV lounge. Trekking, plantation tours and bike rentals are offered.

title: WelcomHeritage Fernhills Royal Palace
description: Opulent hotel in a 19th-century palace offering lush gardens, antiques-filled suites & fine dining.
address: Ferrnhills Post, Ooty, Tamil Nadu 643004, India
hotelDescription:
Set in a former palace dating from 1844, this opulent all-suite hotel on 20 hectares of landscaped grounds is 2 km from both Udagamandalam train station (part of the Nilgiri Mountain Railway) and boating at Ooty Lake.
 Stately, antiques-filled suites with period features have living rooms and dressing rooms. All offer fireplaces, whirlpool tubs, flat-screen TVs and minibars, as well as tea and coffeemaking facilities.
 Breakfast is served in the ornate, upscale restaurant. Other amenities include a formal wood-paneled bar and a grand ballroom, as well as lush gardens with sitting areas.

ownerUpdates.text: Embark on a journey of luxury, heritage, and rejuvenation at WelcomHeritage Fernhills Royal Palace.
Where heritage meets luxury—experience timeless elegance in every corner. From vintage charm to regal comfort, this is more than a stay—it’s a journey back in time.

Looking for a hotel in Ferrnhills Post, Tamil Nadu?
WelcomHeritage Fernhills Royal Palace offers a majestic stay wrapped in timeless luxury. Set amidst serene greenery, this heritage palace features royal-inspired rooms, warm hospitality, and world-class amenities. Whether you're seeking a peaceful retreat or a royal vacation experience, this iconic property promises unforgettable memories in the heart of the Nilgiris. Book your stay and feel the grandeur of a true palace getaway!

Experience royal dining at Curry & Rice, the in-house multi-cuisine restaurant at WelcomHeritage Fernhills Royal Palace, Ooty. Savor the unique flavors of Anglo-Indian cuisine along with global favorites, all served in a regal setting. Enjoy all-day dining with a poolside dining option that enhances your Ooty luxury experience. Relish fine food, royal ambiance, and warm hospitality that reflect the heritage of this grand palace.

A1. Curry & Rice offers multi-cuisine dishes with a special focus on Anglo-Indian delicacies.

Q2. Is there a poolside dining option available?
A2. Yes, guests can enjoy all-day dining with a poolside seating experience.

Q3. Where is Curry & Rice located?
A3. It is the in-house restaurant at WelcomHeritage Fernhills Royal Palace, Ooty.

Q4. What makes dining at Curry & Rice special?
A4. The restaurant combines heritage charm, royal ambiance, and authentic flavors for a luxury dining experience.

Q5. Is the restaurant open to non-resident guests?
A5. Yes, walk-in guests are welcome to dine and enjoy the exquisite atmosphere.

Celebrate grand events at WelcomHeritage Fernhills Royal Palace, Ooty! With a royal wedding hall, lush lawn, and a well-equipped boardroom, it’s the perfect venue for weddings, conferences, and special occasions.

Step into royal elegance at WelcomHeritage Fernhills Royal Palace — the Best Heritage Palace Hotel Stay with an Amazing Experience in Fernhills, Ooty. Stay amid timeless architecture, lush gardens, and luxurious comfort for a truly regal getaway. 

Q1. Where is WelcomHeritage Fernhills Royal Palace located?
A. We are in Fernhills, Ooty, surrounded by scenic hills and serene landscapes.

Q2. What makes this heritage palace hotel special?
A. Its royal architecture, vintage interiors, lush gardens, and a rich history create an unforgettable heritage experience.

Q3. Does the hotel have a restaurant?
A. Yes guests can enjoy delicious multi-cuisine dining in a royal ambiance.

Q4. Is the palace hotel suitable for weddings or events?
A. Absolutely — it’s an iconic venue for weddings, royal gatherings, and elegant celebrations.

Q5. Are nearby tourist attractions easily accessible?
A. Yes, we are close to Ooty Lake, Doddabetta Peak, Botanical Gardens, and other popular spots.

reviews.text:
Hi , Dear all
My name is Bharati Balkrishna Mahajan.
Our native place Vaigani. No one can allow to stay in this village from 23 to 26th Feb. They introduce as a Gaopalan.
So we plan to stay at Tondavali Talashil Chivarion Resort.
First I thanks to Owner of this Resort.
Secondly i thanks to his complete staff and Team who gave us service very well. They kept this Resort ,their kitchen , evrything clean and neat.
Kitchen also kept clean. VEG AND NON VEG food is very tasty . They cooked there fresh food and serve you until your satisfaction. Here You feeled  as you stay in your home only.
We stay there 3 nights.We enjoyed it.
The rooms built by Bamboo (chivari).
All construction is in Bamboo .You can sit in your room and enjoy the Sunlight , Sunset, peacful atmosphere, high jump water waves, all are meeting and welcome you.
Here three side water and in middle people are लिव्हिंग.
I really appreciate each and every staff.
We enjoying and today 26th Feb our Gaopalan finish at 12pm, but we decide to stay here one more night

Thanks And Regards

reviews.name: kunal
reviews.text:
Had a wonderful stay at Chivarin Resort. The sea view room was absolutely beautiful—waking up to the sound of waves made the experience extra special. 🌊

The Malvani food was delicious and authentic, full of flavor and freshly prepared. Service was quick and well-managed, and the staff was extremely polite, helpful, and attentive throughout our stay.

Overall, a peaceful place with great hospitality, tasty food, and a stunning sea view. Highly recommended for anyone looking to relax and enjoy quality service.
reviews.responseFromOwnerText: Thank you Kunal for your amazing feedback. Please do visit again with family and friends 🙏

reviews.text: We had a very disappointing experience at this hotel. There was a clear lack of professionalism throughout our stay. title: Amritara Ambatty Greens Resort, Coorg
description: Modern hotel on a golf course, offering free breakfast, plus a restaurant, a bar & a pool.
address: Bitangalla, near Coorg Golf links, Virajpet, Karnataka 571218, India
hotelDescription:
This relaxed resort is an 8-minute walk from Coorg Golf Links, 5 km from the Evergreen County resort and 21 km from the Porcupine Castle resort.
 The modern rooms come with desks and flat-screen TVs with satellite channels. They also feature sitting areas and balconies with golf course views, as well as minibars and tea and coffeemaking facilities. Children under 5 stay free.
 The resort offers free parking and a breakfast buffet. Additional amenities include an international restaurant and a casual bar. There's also a golf course and an infinity pool, plus a library, an on-site doctor and banquet and conference halls.

ownerUpdates.text: Celebrate love this Valentine’s Weekend with a romantic escape at Amritara Hotels & Resorts. Enjoy a cozy stay, elegant ambience, and memorable moments with your special someone from 13–15 February. Reserve your Valentine getaway today.

Atithyam by Amritara — more than a loyalty programme, it’s a promise.

A promise to celebrate your journey, cherish your choices, and make every return feel like homecoming.
From curated experiences to thoughtful upgrades, each benefit is our way of saying: we notice you, we value you, and we’re grateful you’re here.

Because at Amritara, every stay is more than a visit — it’s your story, with you at its heart.

In the heart of Virajpet, where rolling greens kiss misty hills, lies a retreat made for seekers of peace and beauty.

From intimate gazebo dinners to heartfelt hospitality and the melody of Coorg’s birdsong, Ambatty Greens is more than a stay — it’s a story of nature, nurture, and timeless calm.
Here, silence is not empty; it’s full of meaning.

Escape to Coorg, where every sunrise, sip, and smile becomes a memory you’ll carry forever.

Amritara Ambatty Greens Resorts blends serene golf-course views, lush greens, and laid-back luxury to create a true escape. From peaceful mornings to cozy evenings, every moment here feels calm, refreshing, and effortlessly stylish—perfect for a soulful getaway.

Escape to pure luxury at Amritara Ambatty Greens Resort, the best pool resort in Coorg for vacations. Surrounded by lush greenery and breathtaking views of the golf course, the resort offers elegant rooms, a serene outdoor pool, and world-class hospitality. Whether you're planning a romantic getaway or a peaceful family holiday, Amritara Ambatty Greens promises relaxation, nature, and unforgettable moments in the heart of Coorg. Book your perfect vacation retreat today!

Wake up to dew-fresh mornings, endless stretches of green, and nature whispering peace in every direction.
A serene hideaway where every moment feels calm, pure, and beautifully unhurried. 🌿✨

Host your dream wedding in Coorg at one of the region’s finest resorts, where nature and celebration come together beautifully. Surrounded by the lush greenery of coffee plantations, this venue offers a breathtaking backdrop for every ceremony — from engagement and mehendi to reception and grand wedding functions. With spacious event areas, personalized arrangements, and warm, guest-friendly staff, your special day becomes truly unforgettable. Celebrate love amidst nature’s most serene views in Coorg.

FAQs
Q1. Can the resort host all wedding ceremonies?
Ans. Yes, the resort can accommodate engagements, mehendi, receptions, parties, and full wedding celebrations.

Q2. Is the venue suitable for large gatherings?
Ans. Yes, it offers spacious indoor and outdoor areas ideal for both intimate and grand weddings.

Q3. Do you offer assistance with wedding planning?
Ans. Yes, the team provides support for décor, arrangements, and event coordination.

Q4. What views can guests enjoy during the wedding?
Ans.Guests can enjoy stunning natural views of lush green coffee plantations.

Q5. Is the staff trained for hosting big events?
Ans. Yes, the resort has a warm, professional, and highly guest-friendly team.

Savour multiple cuisines at Arabasta, the in-house restaurant at Amritara Ambatty Greens Resort, Coorg! Enjoy a fine dining experience with a variety of flavours and some of the best beverages, all in a serene and scenic setting.

Stay at Amritara Ambatty Greens Resort, Coorg – the best resort in Coorg, the Scotland of India, surrounded by misty hills and lush coffee plantations of Western Karnataka. A dream escape for every coffee lover!

title: Zostel Munnar
description: Simple dorms & rooms in a hip hostel offering dining, a lounge & a terrace with tea field views.
address: 2nd Mile, Pallivasal, Munnar, Kerala 685565, India
hotelDescription:
Featuring views of the surrounding tea fields, this trendy hostel off the NH85 road is 4 km from Attukad Waterfalls, and 8 km from tours and tastings at Kolukkumalai Tea Estate.
 The simple mixed-gender and female-only dorms provide Wi-Fi, bunk beds and shared bathrooms. Linens are included, and towels can be rented. Private rooms offer en suite bathrooms, and balconies with tea field views.
 Amenities include a restaurant, a colourful guest lounge, and a rooftop terrace with views of the fields. Breakfast and parking are available.

title: Savoy, Ooty - IHCL SeleQtions
subTitle: சவோய், ஊட்டி - ஐக் செலக்ஷன்ஸ்
description: Chic mountain retreat featuring refined dining, an Ayurvedic spa & complimentary breakfast.
address: 77, Sylks Rd, Monterosa Colony, Ooty, Tamil Nadu 643001, India
hotelDescription:
Set in a series of 19th-century cottages nestled among 2.5 hectares of genteel gardens and rolling hills, this upscale hotel is 4.5 km from the Gymkhana Golf Course and 9 km from Doddabetta mountain.
 The sophisticated rooms and suites, with traditional wood decor, high ceilings and subdued tones, feature balconies, free Wi-Fi, TVs and minibars, as well as coffeemakers; most have fireplaces. Room service is available 24/7.
 Parking and breakfast are complimentary. A lounge offers formal dining; there's also a casual restaurant and an elegant fireside bar, plus a 24-hour gym, an Ayurvedic spa and outdoor sports facilities.

reviews.name: Shwetam Khabiya
reviews.text:
Our stay at Savoy, Ooty was truly memorable, and we are deeply grateful for the warm and gracious hospitality extended to us. A special thank you to Manager Titus Vinod, Sabarish and team  for their exceptional care and attention to detail, which made our experience feel personal and welcoming.

We would also like to appreciate the restaurant chef and the entire team for serving us with delicious food and impeccable hospitality throughout our stay.

The property itself is a beautiful reflection of heritage charm — minimalist yet elegant in every sense. Its peaceful atmosphere, surrounded by abundant nature, creates a rare sense of calm and timelessness.

Savoy is not just a place to stay, but an experience that stays with you. We look forward to returning again.

Use of the pool is subject to our pool rules and safety guidelines, designed to ensure a clean and enjoyable experience for all guests.
Please consult our pool schedule at reception or on your room’s information directory.

reviews.name: Soumya Mukherjee
reviews.text:
Location Can’t Save a Sinking Ship
If real estate is the only thing that matters to you, Hotel Maurya Bhubaneswar has it in spades. Positioned perfectly for anyone needing to navigate the heart of the city, its location is—quite literally—its only saving grace. Unfortunately, once you step through the front doors, the experience goes downhill faster than a "dropped packet" of data.
While the rooms are decent and well-maintained, they are overshadowed by service standards that feel less like hospitality and more like an endurance test.
The "Service" (Or Lack Thereof)
The room service here can only be described as abysmal. Whether it’s a lack of training or a lack of staff, the result is a frustratingly slow and unresponsive experience. In a city known for its warmth, the service here feels cold and indifferent.
Bizarre Rules & Logistics
The hotel operates under a set of "house rules" that defy modern logic:
The Tea/Coffee Curfew: Good luck getting a caffeine fix in the evening. The hotel has a baffling rule prohibiting tea or coffee service after 5:30 PM.
Early Kitchen Closure: The kitchen shuts down at 9:30 PM, which is remarkably early for a city hotel. If you are arriving late from a long day of sightseeing or work, expect to go hungry or rely on delivery apps.
The Ice-less Bar: In perhaps the most confusing twist of all, the bar—an establishment designed to serve chilled drinks—does not have ice cubes. Trying to enjoy a drink here is a lesson in lukewarm disappointment.
A Groundhog Day Breakfast
While breakfast is included, don't expect variety. The menu repeats identically every single day. By day three, the lack of innovation becomes a chore rather than a meal.
The Verdict
The universe may not have an intent to harm us, but this hotel’s management certainly seems intent on testing your patience.
Pros: * Excellent, central location.
Decent, clean rooms.
Cons: * Frustratingly restricted service hours.
Repetitive dining options.
A general lack of basic amenities (like ice).
Final Word: Stay here only if you absolutely must be in this specific neighborhood and plan on spending every waking hour—including mealtimes—somewhere else.

title: Kalyan Guest House Hampi

reviews.name: Mark Hammond
reviews.text: Excellent lodging in a great location in the special little town of Hampi. My room was large, clean, comfortable, and everything worked as it should; the property also has a large patio with outdoor seating for guests to enjoy. The owner is a great guy who will help ensure you have a rewarding stay. It's a short walk to the main temple, and ancient ruins up Hematuka Hill and down the Hampi Bazaar road. Hampi is pretty much no cars allowed, making it easy to stroll through town and relish its unique ambiance. Coffee, tea, and water are complimentary at Kalyan; for breakfast just go across the street or around the corner. I booked two nights initially and extended to a third given the many things to see and do around Hampi, and the pleasant accommodation I enjoyed at Kalyan Guest House. In the pic, Kalyan is on the left... and look at that boulder!

reviews.name: Kundan Chavhan
reviews.text:
Wonderful stay!
The owner was extremely helpful and kind. My little daughter was insisting on eating Maggi, and they kindly allowed us to prepare it for her, which meant a lot to us.
The room was clean and well-maintained, with hot water available at any time.
The location is excellent—very close to Virupaksha Temple and easily reachable by walking.
Overall, a very comfortable and pleasant stay. Highly recommended!

No Crackers.
Because what we offer is far more rare.

Real Silence that heals
Conversations that matter
Mornings with birds, not alarms
Food that energizes, not excites temporarily
Air you can actually breathe
Time where phones rest and hearts connect

This picture is not about a crowd.

It’s about a collective longing.

A longing for real love.
For family time without distraction.
For energy that comes from nature, not caffeine.
For food cooked with care, not chemicals.
For a toxic-free life.
For human connection over digital noise.
People don’t come here to escape life.
They come here to remember what life truly is.

Thank you to every soul who chose presence over partying,
connection over chaos,
and meaning over momentary pleasure.
This retreat is not for everyone.

It is for those who feel something missing…
and are finally ready to come back to themselves 
— Evergreen County Resort - India's First Adventure Retreat 
Your Transformation is a call away.... 9742368143 / 7760090007

reviews.name: ram
reviews.text:
Wow!wow! and wow!
Evergreen county resort is an idyllic all inclusive and perfect Vegetarian resort.
Right from the time we landed we were greeted by Vithan and guided to our room.
All amenities are top notch.The fresh crisp air is palpable. You instantly feel relaxed and settle into a tranquil state of mind. Akash and Shagi were supportive.

Fresh delicious 😋 wholesome Vegetarian home made food served on time made it all the more enticing.  You feel connected to nature which induces a sense of peace and energy.
On-site activities like zipline,balance bridge, kayaking and coracle was awesome. Darshan guided us professionally and the family had a blast!
Puneet and Bharat took us on an unforgettable night trail walk through the coffee estate under the  moonlight and stars explaining the process of how coffee beans come alive. Inaddition various other native plants and trees used in our cusines and how our ancestors used food as medicine. Holistic view on nature and elements and how it shapes our health in flesh and spirit was inspiring.
Every single staff was so welcoming and catered to our needs with enthusiasm. They guided us to plan our other daytrips and provided timely support.
It was an absolute pleasure to meet the owner Mr. Bajan Bopanna who shared his vision and the ideas behind    how he started the resort.
We are definitely coming back for more experiences.
Stay tuned!

Horrible experience on the house boat.
Please avoid staying here.

Hygiene standards are very poor , the bathroom doesn't function properly. You can't use the shower and the sink as the water is dirty , there is no pressure in the water.
You'll be asked to use the washroom in a room at the complex where the boat is parked which is very inconvenient.

reviews.text: Fabulous staff and food ! Absolutely loved the scenery! Took two smaller boat trips along the little canals which was organised by the captain and they were outstanding experiences.

reviews.name: Y U
reviews.text:
Depuis plus de 30 ans je rêvais de venir en Inde du Sud et de passer au moins 24h sur un bateau backwaters au Kérala pour découvrir les canaux, leurs village, la faune.
Mon agent de voyage m’a conseillé de réserver un bateau géré par les hôtels Abad. Pourquoi pas?
Le départ et l’arrivée du bateau ont lieu à l’hôtel Abad whispering palms.
Première déception : à mon arrivée à la réception de l’hôtel, point de départ de la mini croisière, , on m’ explique que la navigation se ferait uniquement de 12h à  17h. Ensuite le bateau serait amarré à l’hôtel, le repas du soir et le petit déjeuner seront servi à bord du bateau.
Je pensais vraiment passer la nuit au milieu du lac ou dans un canal Comme c’est le cas par exemple quand on réserve un bateau privé ven baie d’halong au Vietnam ou l’on passe vraiment 24h sur l’eau.

La promenade sur le lac est peu interessante : on n’y voit ni village ni faune.
Pour voir les canaux et les habitations locales il faut payer un supplément et monter dans une barque à moteur. Le tour de 45mn coûte 1000rp par personne

Même si cela est court cette promenade a sauvé un peu notre journée de la catastrophe, il était bon de voir la population locale vivre au bord de ces canaux. Par contre cela n’a rien de sauvage.

Heureusement notre capitaine qui était particulièrement gentil nous a proposé de rentrer au soleil couchant . À ce moment de la journée nous avons pu apercevoir quelques oiseaux.

Le bateau lui même était assez ancien mais joli extérieurement et intérieurement. Malheureusement la salle de bain était en très mauvais état:le tuyau de douche était cassé , l’eau n’arrivait pas dans le pommeau et l’eau était froide .

Les repas étaient corrects: pas très copieux mais bons.

Au final j’ai trouvé le rapport qualité / prix plutôt mauvais.

Je pense aussi que le sur-tourisme à eu des effets catastrophiques sur la région et ce que l’on peut lire dans les brochures sur l’écho system de cette région me semble  très  loin de la réalité. La région m’a énormément déçue par rapport à ce que j’en attendais

En fait plutôt que le house boat, le meilleur moyen pour découvrir les backwaters et ses lagunes est de louer une pirogue motorisée ou, pour une balade plus sportive, en kayak. Ces deux modes de navigation vous permettent d'accéder aux parties reculées des backwaters et d'être, par conséquent, au plus près de la nature et de la vie des villages qui les bordent. Hélas je ne le savais pas avant de réserver cette péniche
reviews.textTranslated:
For over 30 years I'd dreamed of coming to South India and spending at least 24 hours on a backwater boat in Kerala to explore the canals, their villages, and the wildlife.
My travel agent advised me to book a boat operated by Abad Hotels. Why not?
The boat departs from and arrives at the Abad Whispering Palms hotel.
My first disappointment: upon arriving at the hotel reception, the starting point of the mini-cruise, I was told that the boat trip would only run from 12 pm to 5 pm. After that, the boat would be moored at the hotel, and dinner and breakfast would be served on board.
I really thought I'd be spending the night in the middle of the lake or in a canal, like when you book a private boat in Halong Bay in Vietnam, where you truly spend 24 hours on the water.

The boat trip on the lake was rather uninteresting: you see neither villages nor wildlife. To see the canals and local houses, you have to pay extra and board a motorboat. The 45-minute tour costs 1000 rupees per person.

Even though it was short, this trip salvaged our otherwise disastrous day. It was nice to see the local people living along the canals. However, it wasn't exactly wild.

Fortunately, our captain, who was particularly kind, offered to take us back at sunset. At that time of day, we were able to spot some birds.

The boat itself was quite old but attractive both inside and out. Unfortunately, the bathroom was in very poor condition: the shower hose was broken, the water didn't reach the showerhead, and the water was cold.

The meals were decent: not very substantial, but tasty.

In the end, I found the value for money rather poor.

I also think that overtourism has had catastrophic effects on the region, and what you read in brochures about the region's ecosystem seems very far from reality. The region disappointed me enormously compared to my expectations.

In fact, rather than a houseboat, the best way to discover the backwaters and their lagoons is to rent a motorized dugout canoe or, for a more adventurous trip, a kayak. These two modes of transport allow you to access the remote parts of the backwaters and, consequently, to be as close as possible to nature and the life of the villages that line them. Unfortunately, I didn't know this before booking the houseboat.

reviews.name: D S
reviews.text:
We travelled as a family with two kids and stayed at Abad Cruise – Houseboat on 1st October 2025, booking the one-bedroom private houseboat for our family.

One of the key advantages of choosing this houseboat was that it’s linked to Abad Whispering Palms Resort, Kumarakom, which allowed us to park our car safely at the resort and use its facilities like the swimming pool and play area when the boat docked in the evening — a major plus point for families with kids.

Everything was well-organized from the start. The staff shared the meal plan and cruise schedule in advance, including the exact route and timings. We were welcomed at the resort with refreshing drinks and guided to the jetty for boarding.

Once on board, the excitement was palpable, especially for the kids. The houseboat was clean and comfortable, with AC in both the bedroom and living area. The bed was cozy, ensuring a good night’s rest after a long day. The TV wasn’t used, and the only minor issue was the poor mobile network connectivity, which is quite expected in the backwaters.

The onboard staff were friendly and professional, especially Captain Amal Dev, who stood out for his politeness and warm interactions. He even allowed our kids to “be the captain” for a few minutes, which absolutely thrilled them!

The food cooked onboard was delightful — a highlight of the trip. Authentic Kerala flavors - particularly the fish fry was simply delicious. In the evening, we were served pazham pori and tea, adding a perfect local touch.

The cruise through the Vembanad Lake was serene and scenic, passing paddy fields, village homes, and flocks of cormorants. Around 2:30 pm, the boat stopped for the staff’s lunch break, during which we took a canoe ride through narrow canals. It was a beautiful experience witnessing lakeside village life and even spotting floating shops selling tender coconuts and ice cream — giving off charming Vietnam/Thailand vibes.

Our houseboat sailed until 5:00 pm, with the option to extend till 6:00 pm instead of doing the morning ride. We opted for the evening extension, during which we stopped by a local fish stall near the paddy fields to buy fresh catch for dinner.

By 6:00 pm, the boat docked back at Abad Whispering Palms Resort, where we spent the evening enjoying the swimming pool, play area, and indoor games like table tennis and badminton. To top it off, there was a live classical music concert at the resort — a soothing end to a memorable day.

Dinner back on the boat was fantastic, especially the freshly cooked fish. The next morning, however, the only downside was the staff’s insistence on an early checkout, repeatedly prompting us from 7 am despite the official time being 9 am. This was quite unnecessary and slightly soured an otherwise perfect experience.

Overall, Abad Cruise – Houseboat offered a wonderful mix of comfort, hospitality, and authentic Kerala backwater charm. Our kids loved every bit of it, and the tie-up with Abad Whispering Palms made the stay even more enjoyable. Highly recommended for families looking for a peaceful and memorable backwater experience in Kumarakom!

reviews.name: Sakshi Dangayach
reviews.text: Had the most amazing experience at Swaswara. For the first time, we did a wellness retreat and it was the best! Staff was very warm, friendly and professional. Food was great, there were many wellness activities to do and the resort itself was beyond beautiful. Beach was just 5 mins away, swimming pool was pristine, rooms were so comfortable. The staff ensured the stay was absolutely wonderful at every point. Would definitely visit again :)

reviewContext.Hotel highlights: Luxury, Great view, Quiet, Great value

reviews.name: Lindsey Angisetty
reviews.text:
My husband and I had a fantastic week-long stay at SwaSwara. The villa was beautiful - we loved the open roof concept for maximum fresh air (bedroom and bathroom, except shower, are enclosed). Taking a shower in the open air with the purified rain water was a healing experience in itself. My hair and skin feel so nice after one week without chemicals and harsh water. All the amenities one needs are included, including a hair dryer.

The overall location and surroundings is ideal for wellness. My favorite place on the grounds was the Meditation Hill which overlooks Om Beach and is a serene oasis for yoga, meditation or just chilling and watching the birds and beach action. Lots of fun nature watching anywhere on the grounds (non-people bothering monkeys and lots of cute squirrels, frogs, birds and even a peacock).

The food is perfect for my palate - healthy, mild spice (you can specify mild, med, spicy for the general stay) and vegetarian plus fish. I generally only eat two meals a day at home but didn’t want to miss anything the chefs lovingly prepared with the veggies from the garden on the property, so I ended up eating quite a bit (for me) without gaining any weight and feeling great. The chefs are incredibly accommodating for any food allergies and preferences.

My husband and I had consultations with the Ayurvedic doctors at the beginning of the trip (even though we weren’t doing the specific Ayurvedic program) and received a custom massage treatment plan for the week. The massages were very relaxing and rejuvenating - thoroughly enjoyed them. The doctors were very helpful and caring.

I felt all of the staff went above and beyond to make sure we were happy and well taken care of. With only 24 villas, it was never busy or crowded, but there were also activities that brought people together if you want to meet people and make a few new friends. Would highly recommend joining a cooking class and making some pottery in the art studio, of course in addition to any yoga or meditation session.

Next time, I would like to try the Ayurvedic program or even the 21 day Panchakarma program which is more strict with the food and treatment options and also activities you can do (no swimming or exploring the town). I spoke with several people who had done these programs, and they said they felt completely rejuvenated (and their faces were literally glowing!) This is a gem of a place. Yes, the price tag is certainly higher than other Ayurvedic centers, but it felt more like a vacation than a hospital stay and that makes the extra cost worth it. We will definitely be back!

reviews.text:
An absolutely unforgettable stay at Red Earth, Gokarna! 🌊🌴 The property is stunning, peaceful, and beautifully blends with nature. Being so close to the beach, waking up to the sound of waves and the serene surroundings made the experience truly magical.

reviews.name: Shalini Roy
reviews.text:
Hii. I am Shalini Roy. My Sister-in-law and I stayed in the property from 1st February to 5th February. It was overall a good stay. The staffs were helpful and they arranged whatever we needed. It was a safe stay. But there were some issues we want to mention specifically. First of all the property was not well maintained. It was not clean and not like the other property of Red Earth. Second when we gave them our room keys for cleaning when we were out, I found my bracelet was missing after returning. I informed them then and there and after 15-20 mins they came with the bracelet and informed me that by mistakenly they took it. I didn't expect that. And because it had elastic, I found that it got loose. N third when we asked for one particular person as our airport drop driver, first they told that they will inform but later they neither assign him nor inform us. Highly dissatisfied with this behaviour.
But overall whenever we asked for any help or room service they were there. The food was good and the other services were fine. Thank you!
reviews.responseFromOwnerText:
Thank you for taking the time to share your detailed feedback, Shalini. We're genuinely glad your stay felt safe, that the team was helpful when you needed them, and that you enjoyed the food.

That said, we sincerely regret the concerns you've raised. On the matter of your bracelet - it was inadvertently picked up along with the bed linen and sent to the laundry. The moment this was noticed, it was returned to you immediately. We understand how unsettling that must have felt, and our team on the ground is deeply apologetic. We have since had a firm conversation with the housekeeping staff to ensure all linen is carefully checked before being moved to the laundry room - this should not happen again.

We also take note of your feedback regarding the upkeep and cleanliness of the property, as well as the miscommunication around your airport transfer request. These are not the standards we hold ourselves to, and we are addressing both with the team directly.

Your trust means a great deal to us, Shalini. Feedback like yours - specific and honest - is what helps us get better. We truly hope to have the chance to welcome you and your sister-in-law back and give you the experience that reflects the best of Red Earth Gokarna.

Team Red Earth
Kabini | Tadoba | Gokarna | Nilgiris | Wayanad

subTitle: ஹோட்டல் மயூரா சுதர்சன்
description: Unassuming rooms with cable TV in a no-nonsense hotel offering a modest restaurant & a garden.

street: Lake Rd, opp. Good Shepherd International School Campus
hotelDescription:
On a landscaped property amid the Nilgiri Hills, this straightforward hotel in a low-rise building is a 15-minute walk from Fernhill train station and 6 km from the 22-hectare Government Botanical Garden.
 Unpretentious rooms feature cable TV. Some have colourful artwork and/or exposed brick walls. Room service is available.
 Amenities include an unassuming restaurant and a garden. Parking is available; a generator is on-site.
