reviews.name: KOMAL JADHAV
reviews.text:
We stayed at Hotel Mayura Sudarshan from 20th to 23rd February, and our experience was extremely disappointing.

Before booking through MMT, we specifically called the hotel reception and clearly requested Rooms 4, 5, or 6, as we had seen them online and they suited our family’s needs. The staff confirmed our request over the phone, and based on that assurance, we proceeded with the booking.

However, upon arrival, we were allotted completely different rooms. The reason given was that the hotel was “full.” Shockingly, during our entire 3-night stay, only one other room appeared to be occupied. This explanation did not seem genuine.

We repeatedly informed the reception that we were traveling with small children and had specifically enquired in advance about those particular rooms. Despite this, our request was outright refused.

The room given to us was not clean.

Towels provided had visible stains.

Walls had noticeable seepage issues.

Overall hygiene and maintenance were poor.

When we expressed our dissatisfaction and mentioned that we had somehow adjusted for two nights but wanted to cancel the third night with a refund, the staff’s response was extremely unprofessional. The bearded man at reception (possibly named Sadanand) was arrogant and dismissive. Instead of addressing our concern, he simply said, “If you want to go, checkout is at 11,” and walked away while we were still speaking. Completely unacceptable behavior.

As for the food — it’s better to eat outside. You will find far better taste and variety elsewhere in Ooty.

The only positive aspect of this hotel is the garden and the view. Unfortunately, that alone cannot compensate for poor service, lack of cleanliness, and unprofessional staff behavior.

Overall, a very bad experience. We would not recommend this hotel to families, especially those traveling with children.

reviews.name: abhijeet kamerkar
reviews.text:
Our stay at Ramada by Wyndham Alleppey was absolutely wonderful and truly memorable. The hotel offers excellent hospitality and a beautiful ambience that instantly makes you feel relaxed and welcomed.

The property is very well maintained, clean, and equipped with great facilities including a basketball court, swimming pool, and badminton court. Everything was perfectly organized and spotless.

The food was simply delicious with a wide variety of cuisines to choose from – every meal was a delight! Our room was spacious, comfortable, and equipped with all the modern amenities needed for a pleasant stay.

A special mention to the amazing staff who were always smiling, courteous, and ready to help. Front Desk Devika ma’am and Duty Manager Sofiya ma’am were extremely helpful, soft-spoken, and guided us with all the necessary information about the hotel facilities.

Overall, it was one of the best hotel experiences we have ever had. We truly wish we could have stayed a couple more days! Highly recommended for anyone visiting Alleppey who wants comfort, great service, and a memorable stay.👌👌

reviews.text:
Lovely property in the woods away from the hustle and bustle of the city. We took the Cottage Package and extended it by two nights. Even spending a day resting on the room was nice because it is so serene.

Accommodation is spacious and clean but more functional than luxurious. When you factor in 2.5 days of sight seeing in their own jeep and all meals including evening tea and snacks thrown in, it is very good value for money too.

Food served is homely, staff are very friendly and helpful with a very relaxed demeanour overall.

It is a large complex built on a hill, so your cottage can be a bit far from the restaurant. Something for those with mobility issues to be aware of.

reviewContext.Rooms: Large, clean and functional. Not luxury.
reviewContext.Nearby activities: Hampi attractions are about 6-8 kms away.
reviewContext.Walkability: Built on a hill. Not recommended for people with mobility issues.
reviewContext.Noteworthy details:
Not all rooms have views, only those on top of the hill… Cottages, 18-19 and 20.

It is out of town, so expect to pay over the odds to taxi and autos to get here. We paid Rs. 500 for the auto ride from Hosapete station.

title: Bamboo Lagoon Boutique Resort
description: Quaint, waterfront resort offering colonial-style bungalows, free use of canoes & 24/7 room service.
address: Nehrutrophy Ward, Punnamada, Finishing Point, Alappuzha, Mullakkal, Kerala 688006, India
hotelDescription:
Set on the banks of the Kollam-Kottapuram Waterway, this relaxed, tropical resort occupies a cluster of colonial-style bungalows. It's 6 km from Alappuzha lighthouse, 7 km from Alappuzha Beach and 15 km from Pathiramanal Island (famed for rare birds).
 Simple rooms come with wooden furnishings, cable TV and water views. Room service is provided 24/7.
 Use of canoes is complimentary, while motor boat rentals, Ayurvedic treatments and houseboats excursions are available for a fee.

reviews.name: Swai Maheshwari
reviews.text: Good stay!!

reviews.name: Tim Brooks
reviews.text:
The arrival route makes one doubt whether they’re going the right way, but those doubts are dispelled the moment the gates of the property are flung open. A short drive up and you are taken through the front doors, revealing a truly special view onto the estuary, beyond which Belekan beach lies.

It’s the perfect place to relax and just let yourself be taken care of. If you want some light activity, you can walk to either of the nearby beaches, or use the jungle tennis (best backdrop of any tennis court I’ve played on!).

The staff are amazing, be sure to ask any questions or for any arrangements you need, including for getting to or leaving from the place (don’t make the mistake I made and not look at your phone the day of arrival, they will arrange to meet you at the nearby village). A special mention to Preema, who is such a lovely person and will sort out anything you need at the drop of a WhatsApp message. Thank you Preema and the rest of the Kahani staff!

reviews.name: Sally Holbrook
reviews.text:
Not necessarily the physical property itself was my issue, it was other things that unfortunately ruined my days in Hampi. I would like to share that I have travelled a huge amount, to over 90 countries, and it is very rare for me to encounter what I did at Rocky’s. Normally I stay in dorm rooms at hostels, but decided to treat myself in Hampi, which was clearly a mistake. Never before have I been treated so badly.

I also need to mention that the place was extremely noisy. This was not due to guests or the road, but the staff themselves, who were shouting very loudly outside my room early in the morning. It was not occasional noise, it was loud and disruptive.

The main issue however was their Tuk Tuk driver, Eggy. While he may not technically be staff, he is effectively treated as such and clearly works closely with the property. He came and picked me up from the bus, which was no problem. The next day we agreed he would take me on a tour from 9am to 6pm, and we agreed a price of 1,600 rupees. I told him several times that I am very quick at going around places, but he insisted it would take nine hours. I agreed.

At the first temple I came out and could not see him. There were several Tuk Tuks there. He started shouting at me in Hindi to attract my attention, but I cannot understand the language, so I sent him a WhatsApp message. When I finally saw him, after maybe two minutes, he just kept talking at me. When I said I did not understand what he was saying and that I was sorry, he started to mimic me, no kidding, saying things like “I don’t understand Hindi” in a very sarcastic tone. This behaviour continued. Every time I said anything he repeated it back to me in a sarcastic and mimicking way, taking the Mickey out of my accent.

After about an hour of this I was nearly in tears, and I do not cry. I was so humiliated. I finished the whole tour in four hours, yet he still expected to charge me the full amount, even though the distance covered was not far in total. I then asked if we could do another area since I had technically agreed a price for the whole day. I would have been happy to pay the additional fuel cost, but he just shouted at me, called Rocky, and became even angrier.

In the end I paid 1,000 rupees, which was more than half of what we agreed. He was not happy. I got out of the Tuk Tuk and walked the rest of the way.

I would not stay here again and would strongly suggest staying elsewhere. For example, the place directly opposite has fabulous food and great temple views from the rooftop, and would have been a much better choice. It is called Gopi - and the people I spoke to who stayed there loved it! Book there 😊
reviews.responseFromOwnerText:
i don't know what to write. first of all thank you for sharing your experience in our place.
     Regarding the noise early morning, we the staff do pooja and pray to the God. We do bell sound while doing pray to the god and not any noise things which disturbs for the guest. I apologise for the that.
   Also,the new guest arrived with 2 kids quite early in the morning and they were screaming and crying too. Even not only you have made complaint also the other guest who stayed their they also complained about that. I apologise once again.
    regarding the tuk tuk , as requested  we have just sent for pick up from Hospet to Hampi. that's it.
    regarding Hampi sightseeing we have not organised any tuk tuk from the guesthouse. 
      On your own you have contacted and agreed with tuk tuk driver for Hampi sightseeing to visit places in Hampi.
         but i apologise as you got a bad experience with the tuk tuk driver in Hampi. the price that you have agreed on is a very good price, but most of the tourist take atleast 6-7hrs(full day)to see hampi sightseeing and some tourist see the same places in 3-4hrs. it's depends upon the tourist how they like and feel the monuments of Hampi.
       but this is the first time it has happened in the last 25 years of my experience.i will definitely look after it and will make sure everything is good and not have any complaints in future.
   looking forward to your next visit.
best regards

title: Sterling Ooty Elk Hill
description: Functional, modern rooms & suites in a resort with an international restaurant & a 24-hour cafe.
address: No. 25, Ramakrishna Mutt Road, R.K.Puram, Elk Hill, Ooty, Tamil Nadu 643001, India
hotelDescription:
Set in modern brick buildings, this hillside resort is 1.9 km from the Rose Garden, 3.4 km from Ooty Lake and 4 km from the Government Botanical Gardens.
 The warm, contemporary rooms and suites feature wood floors, flat-screen TVs, and tea and coffeemakers. Suites include living rooms with kitchenettes. There are also 1- and 2-bedroom club apartments.
 Breakfast and parking are complimentary. There's an international restaurant and a 24-hour Internet cafe, as well as a kids' playground, a game room and a library. Activities on offer include trekking tours and BBQ dining.

title: The Hosteller Gokarna
description: Bright dorms & private rooms in a laid-back adults-only hostel featuring a cafe & an outdoor pool.
address: 1345/1, Kudle Beach, Gokarna, Karnataka 581326, India
hotelDescription:
Surrounded by forest and paddy fields, this laid-back adults-only hostel is 3 minutes away on foot from Kudle Beach along the Laccadive Sea, 3 km from Gogarbha Cave and 6 km from Gokarna main bus stand.
 Casual, bright mixed-gender and female-only dorms have Wi-Fi and balconies, as well as bunk beds with privacy curtains and reading lights. All offer lockers, air conditioning and shared bathrooms. Private rooms feature TVs, kettles and en suite facilities. Some quarters provide sea views.
 Amenities include a cafe, an outdoor pool, and a terrace with daybeds, plus gardens and lounges. Parking is available.

title: Fortune Resort Sullivan Court, Ooty - Member ITC Hotels’ Group
description: Formal option offering conservative rooms & suites, plus a 24-hour restaurant & landscaped gardens.
address: 123, Selbourne Road, near Rose Garden, Bombay Castel, Ooty, Tamil Nadu 643001, India
hotelDescription:
Set in a former colonial estate, this upscale resort is a 10-minute walk from the Government Rose Garden, 1.6 km from Ooty train station and 5 km from Ooty Golf Course.
 The conservative rooms and suites come with Wi-Fi, flat-screen TVs and minibars, as well as safes, desks, and tea and coffeemaking facilities. Suites add separate sitting areas.
 Parking and a breakfast buffet are complimentary. Other amenities include a refined 24-hour restaurant, a formal bar and a gym, plus landscaped gardens, and a game room with billiards and table tennis tables.

It is especially gratifying to hear that you appreciated the culinary offerings, the personalized attention from our Chef, and the customized dining experience. Creating memorable gastronomic experiences is something we take great pride in.

We are also pleased that you enjoyed the evening tea in the garden and the bonfire experience—these moments are designed to create warmth and engagement, and we are glad they added to your stay.

Most importantly, it was an honor to be a part of your anniversary celebration. Knowing that our team was able to make your special occasion even more memorable is the greatest compliment we could receive.

We look forward to welcoming you back for many more celebrations and serene getaways in Ooty.

Warm Regards

S. Rajan
General Manager

hotelDescription:

hotelDescription:
